# Share Purchase Agreement

**Parties:** Northgate Acquisitions Corp. ("Purchaser"), Riverview Holdings Ltd. ("Vendor"),
and Clearstone Capital Inc. ("Guarantor").

This Share Purchase Agreement is entered into by and among Northgate Acquisitions Corp.,
a corporation incorporated under the laws of Ontario ("Purchaser"), Riverview Holdings Ltd.,
a corporation incorporated under the laws of British Columbia ("Vendor"), and Clearstone
Capital Inc., a corporation incorporated under the laws of Ontario ("Guarantor"), effective
as of January 15, 2026.

## Purchase and Sale

The Vendor agrees to sell, and the Purchaser agrees to purchase, all issued and outstanding
shares of Maplewood Technologies Ltd. (the "Target Shares"), free and clear of all
encumbrances, for an aggregate purchase price of $12,500,000 (the "Purchase Price").

The Purchaser shall pay the Purchase Price as follows:

- A deposit of $1,250,000 shall be paid by the Purchaser to the Vendor's solicitors in trust
  no later than January 22, 2026.
- The balance of $11,250,000 shall be paid by the Purchaser to the Vendor at Closing by
  wire transfer of immediately available funds.

## Closing

The closing of the transactions contemplated herein (the "Closing") shall occur at 10:00 a.m.
on February 28, 2026 (the "Closing Date") at the offices of counsel, or such other date and
time as the Purchaser and Vendor may agree in writing.

Northgate Acquisitions Corp. shall deliver to the Vendor, at or before Closing, the following
closing deliveries:

1. A certified resolution of the board of directors of the Purchaser approving the transaction.
2. An executed officer's certificate confirming the representations and warranties of the
   Purchaser are true and correct as of the Closing Date.
3. Evidence of financing satisfactory to the Vendor, acting reasonably.

## Conditions Precedent

The obligations of the Purchaser to complete the Closing are subject to the following
conditions precedent, each of which may be waived by the Purchaser in writing:

- The representations and warranties of the Vendor set forth on Schedule No. 4 shall be true
  and correct in all material respects as of the Closing Date.
- No material adverse change shall have occurred with respect to the business of Maplewood
  Technologies Ltd. since December 31, 2025.
- All regulatory approvals, including approval under the Competition Act (Canada), shall have
  been obtained on or before February 20, 2026.
- The Vendor shall have delivered all closing documents required under Article 5.

The obligations of the Vendor to complete the Closing are subject to the condition that
the Purchaser shall have paid the deposit described in Section 3.1 prior to Closing.

## Pre-Closing Covenants

From the date hereof until Closing, the Vendor shall cause Maplewood Technologies Ltd. to
operate its business in the ordinary course, consistent with past practice.

The Vendor shall, within 5 business days after written notice from the Purchaser, provide
any information reasonably requested for the Purchaser's due diligence review.

If any representation of the Vendor is found to be materially inaccurate, the Purchaser
shall give written notice to the Vendor specifying the inaccuracy, and the Vendor shall
have 10 business days after receipt of such notice to cure the inaccuracy or the Purchaser
may terminate this Agreement.

## Representations and Warranties

The Vendor represents and warrants to the Purchaser that, as of the date hereof and as of
the Closing Date:

- The Vendor has full authority to enter into this Agreement and to perform its obligations
  hereunder.
- The Target Shares constitute all of the issued and outstanding shares in the capital of
  Maplewood Technologies Ltd., free and clear of any liens, charges, or encumbrances.
- The financial statements of Maplewood Technologies Ltd. are true and correct in all
  material respects and have been prepared in accordance with IFRS.
- There are no legal proceedings pending or threatened against Maplewood Technologies Ltd.
  except as disclosed on Schedule No. 4.
- No consent of any third party is required in connection with the sale of the Target Shares
  except as set forth on Schedule No. 4.

## Indemnification

The Vendor shall indemnify the Purchaser and hold it harmless from and against all losses
arising out of any breach of the representations and warranties of the Vendor contained
herein, provided that any claim for indemnification must be made within 18 months following
the Closing Date.

The maximum aggregate liability of the Vendor under this indemnification shall not exceed
$6,250,000.

## Guarantee

The Guarantor hereby unconditionally and irrevocably guarantees to the Vendor
the due and punctual performance by the Purchaser of all payment obligations of the
Purchaser under this Agreement, up to a maximum aggregate amount equal to the Purchase
Price. The Vendor shall not be required to first pursue any remedy against the Purchaser
before enforcing this guarantee against the Guarantor.

## Governing Law

This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the
Province of Ontario and the federal laws of Canada applicable therein.

Any dispute arising under this Agreement shall be submitted to the exclusive jurisdiction
of the courts of the Province of Ontario.
